articles.title: Scientists Make Tiny Magnetic Waves Last 100 Times Longer — A Big Win for Quantum Technology
articles.summary: Scientists have made an exciting discovery in the world of quantum technology. They have found a way to make magnons — very small magnetic waves inside materials — last nearly 100 times longer than before. In the past, magnons disappeared too quickly to be useful. Now, researchers have extended their lifetime long enough to carry and store quantum information. This could be an important step towards building better quantum computers and communication systems in the future. Quantum technology works very differently from normal computers — it uses the special rules of tiny particles to do things ordinary machines cannot. This breakthrough brings that powerful future technology one step closer to everyday use.

lv1: What are magnons? (磁振子是什麼？)
What did scientists manage to do with magnons in this discovery? (科學家在這次發現中成功對磁振子做了什麼？)
lv2: Why does it matter that magnons can now last 100 times longer? (磁振子能夠持續100倍時間為何如此重要？)
How could this discovery help improve computers or communication systems in the future? (這項發現未來可以如何改善電腦或通訊系統？)
lv3: Quantum computing is still very expensive and complicated. Do you think governments should spend public money on developing it, or should this be left to private companies? Why? (量子運算技術目前仍非常昂貴且複雜。你認為政府應否撥出公共資源研發這項技術，還是應該留給私人企業？請說明你的理由。)

Some people worry that powerful quantum computers could break the security of the internet and banking systems. How should society prepare for this risk, and who should be responsible for doing so? (有人擔心強大的量子電腦可能破解互聯網和銀行系統的保安。社會應如何應對這一風險，又由誰負責？)

articles.title: New Spray Powder Stops Dangerous Bleeding in Just One Second
articles.summary: A team of scientists from KAIST, a well-known university in South Korea, has invented a remarkable new powder that can be sprayed onto a wound to stop serious bleeding in about one second. The powder works by instantly turning into a strong gel when it touches blood, covering the wound tightly and stopping blood loss very quickly. This is especially useful for deep or unusually shaped injuries where older products often fail to work well. Stopping heavy bleeding fast is one of the most important challenges in emergency medicine, and this invention could one day save many lives on battlefields, at accident scenes, or in hospitals. Scientists say the powder is easy to carry and simple to use.

lv1: Where was this new bleeding powder invented? (這種新型止血粉是在哪裡發明的？)
How long does the powder take to stop bleeding? (這種粉末需要多少時間才能止血？)
lv2: Why is it important that this powder can work on deep or oddly shaped wounds? (這種粉末能夠用於深層或形狀不規則的傷口，為何如此重要？)
In what kinds of emergency situations could this new powder be most helpful? (這種新型粉末在哪些緊急情況下最能發揮作用？)
lv3: New medical inventions like this powder often take many years and cost a lot of money to reach patients. Who should be responsible for making sure life-saving technology gets to people quickly — governments, hospitals, or the companies that invent them? (像這種止血粉的新醫療發明，往往需要多年時間及大量資金才能到達病人手中。你認為誰應負責確保救命技術能夠快速惠及大眾——政府、醫院還是發明公司？)

Some advanced medical products are only affordable in rich countries. Is it fair that people in poorer countries may not be able to access life-saving inventions? What could be done to fix this? (一些先進醫療產品只有富裕國家才能負擔。你認為貧窮國家的人無法獲得救命發明是否公平？有什麼方法可以改善這種情況？)

articles.title: 房委會推「特快公屋編配」計劃 7月3日起接受申請
articles.summary: 香港房屋委員會宣布，「特快公屋編配計劃」（2026）將由7月3日起至16日接受申請。合資格人士可透過此計劃提早獲編配公屋單位，亦可選擇購買重售的綠表置居計劃單位或租者置其屋計劃回收單位。此計劃旨在加快公屋輪候時間，讓有需要的家庭更早獲安置。有意申請的市民須符合現行公屋申請資格，並於截止日期前提交申請。

lv1: 「特快公屋編配計劃」由哪一天開始接受申請？
合資格申請人除了可以申請公屋，還可以選擇做什麼？
lv2: 你認為「特快公屋編配計劃」對哪類家庭最有幫助？為什麼？
為什麼香港政府需要推出特快編配計劃來加快公屋分配？
lv3: 有人認為政府應該興建更多公屋以解決住屋問題，也有人認為應該資助市民購買私樓。你支持哪種方法？請提出理由。
公屋資源有限，應該優先分配給哪些人？例如長者、低收入家庭還是輪候時間最長的人？請闡述你的看法。

articles.title: 泰國11歲男童偷駕貨車失控　衝入苦行僧隊伍釀8死多傷
articles.summary: 泰國東北部莫達漢府發生嚴重交通意外。一名年僅11歲的男童在父母不知情下，偷偷駕駛家中的皮卡貨車外出。車輛行駛途中突然失控，直接衝向一支正在路邊進行苦行朝聖的僧侶隊伍。當時隊伍共有35名僧侶及5名信徒，排成縱列在路肩步行。事故造成至少8人死亡、十多人受傷。事發時前方僧侶已嘗試發出警示，但未能及時阻止意外發生。事件引起社會對兒童行車安全及車輛管理的廣泛關注。

lv1: 這宗意外發生在哪個國家？
lv2: 為什麼一名11歲的孩子能夠獨自駕駛貨車？這反映了什麼問題？
這宗意外對苦行僧隊伍造成了什麼影響？
lv3: 有人認為父母應為子女的危險行為負上法律責任，你同意嗎？請從不同角度分析。
如何在社會上推廣兒童安全意識，以減少類似意外的發生？學校、家庭和政府各應扮演什麼角色？
